content: Magnesium chloride is highly bioavailable at 12%, useful for metabolic function and detoxification.
Magnesium citrate interferes with celop plasman, leading to potential iron overload and laxative effects.
Magnesium glycinate is absorbed through the intestine, avoiding laxative effects and providing calming properties.
Magnesium melate enhances ATP production, aiding in energy and muscle contraction, and is a chelated form with higher bioavailability.
Magnesium oxide has low bioavailability and requires daily dosing for sustained release, often bound to fatty acids.
Epsom salt (magnesium sulfate) is not effective for recovery or healing, and may cause laxative effects.
Magnesium orotate penetrates cells, supporting overall cellular health and cardiovascular function.
Magnesium threonate improves short-term and long-term memory, beneficial for anxiety and neurocognitive disorders.
